product_overview: FireDash is a single-page web application for real-time monitoring and management of emergency incidents, providing a role-based dashboard with KPIs, charts, maps and incident CRUD. It uses JWT authentication and supports admin/operator/visualizador roles for access control.
core_goals: Provide a real-time dashboard with KPI cards, charts and an interactive incident table for operational situational awareness
Enforce role-based access (admin, operador, visualizador) so users only perform permitted actions
Allow operators and admins to create, update and delete incidents and maintain accurate incident data
Offer secure authentication and account creation (login/register) with JWT-based sessions
Expose reliable API endpoints for KPIs and incidents and surface errors clearly in the UI

features.name: User Login
features.description: Authenticate users using email and password to gain access to the protected dashboard and app features.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Fill email input field with registered email -> Fill password input field with correct password -> Click Login button to submit -> See Dashboard with KPI cards and incident table

Navigate to / -> Fill email input field with registered email -> Fill password input field with incorrect password -> Click Login button to submit -> See error message 'Invalid credentials' and remain on Login page

features.name: User Registration
features.description: Create a new user account by providing name, email, password and selecting a role; after success the user can sign in.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click Register link to open the registration form -> Fill name field -> Fill email field with a new email -> Fill password field -> Select role -> Click Register -> See success message and be redirected to Login page

Navigate to / -> Click Register link to open the registration form -> Fill name field -> Fill email field with an email that is already registered -> Fill password field -> Select role -> Click Register -> See validation error 'Email already in use' and remain on Register form

features.name: Dashboard & Incidents
features.description: Display KPIs, charts and a filterable incident table; allow permitted users to delete incidents and apply filters to narrow results.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Be authenticated as operador or admin -> See overview stats (KPI cards and charts) -> Change filters (Periodo, Tipo, Gravidade, Status) -> See incident table update to reflect selected filters -> Click Delete button on a specific incident row -> Confirm deletion -> See incident removed from table and success notification
Navigate to / -> Be authenticated as visualizador (read-only) -> See incident table -> Click Delete button on an incident row -> See error 'Insufficient permissions' and incident remains in table

Navigate to / -> Be authenticated -> Change filters (Periodo, Tipo, Gravidade, Status) -> See loading indicator -> API call to /api/incidents fails -> See error message 'Failed to load incidents' and previous data (if any) remains visible

routes.description: Main SPA entry point (conditionally renders Login, Register, or Dashboard)

features.name: User Login
features.description: Login with email and password credentials

features.name: Dashboard & Incidents
features.description: View KPI cards, charts, and filter incident data
